items.title: k-Means partitioning
items.description: k-means fixes k before fitting and optimizes centroids; hierarchical clustering builds a full merge tree so you can choose k afterward by cutting the dendrogram.

items.title: Cluster evaluation metrics
items.description: Silhouette score, Davies–Bouldin index, and cophenetic correlation help judge whether a dendrogram cut yields meaningful, well-separated groups.

items.title: Unsupervised learning paradigm
items.description: Hierarchical clustering discovers structure in unlabeled data by grouping similar observations—core unsupervised pattern discovery without target labels.

items.title: Distance metrics and norms
items.description: Every merge decision depends on pairwise distances (Euclidean, Manhattan, cosine, etc.) and whether features are scaled to comparable units.
